title: Savory Slow Irish Fish Stew
description: A hearty and cozy pescatarian-friendly stew featuring lemongrass and namul-blanch-seasoned fish, perfect for a chilly evening.
instructions: Heat oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onions and cook until they are softened and translucent.
Add the bruised lemongrass stalks and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the sliced carrots and cubed potatoes, cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the cod fillets and cook until they are opaque and flake easily with a fork.
Add the fish stock, heavy cream, salt, and black pepper, stir to combine.
Bring the stew to a simmer, then reduce the heat to low and cook, covered, until the fish is cooked through and the vegetables are tender.
Remove the lemongrass stalks and serve the stew hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
